title: Build forms with the reactive forms model
description: Define form structure and validation in the component as a typed FormGroup rather than scattering logic in the template.
body: Use when a form has validation, dynamic controls, or cross-field rules. Do not drive complex validation through template-only ngModel bindings. Actions: build a typed FormGroup/FormControl tree, attach sync and async validators to controls, react to valueChanges/statusChanges streams (with teardown), and bind controls in the template via formControlName. Validation signals include a test that sets a control value and asserts the group's validity and error map, and that valueChanges emits as expected. Risk boundary: leaving validation only in the template means it is not enforced server-side or in tests; never treat client form validity as a security boundary, and unsubscribe from valueChanges streams.
